ocr.text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of the Asia-Pacific shipping and maritime sector, the interplay of bunker fuel costs and
voyage charter revenue trends has become increasingly critical for stakeholders.

The recent analysis highlights the significant fluctuations in High Sulfur Fuel Oil (HFO) prices, with an emphasis on the current
average cost of SGD 600 per metric ton, which has implications for operational profitability.

As operators navigate these cost pressures, the data reveals that chartered fleets are adapting to market demands, with a
recorded average revenue per fleet of SGD 2M. Furthermore, the newbuild order book status indicates a robust pipeline of 50
total units, signaling a strategic response to anticipated growth in demand.

This combination of factors underscores the necessity for shipping companies to optimize their operational efficiencies and
revenue strategies in a market characterized by volatility. The insights from this analysis will serve as a guiding framework for
decision-making as stakeholders position themselves for sustainable growth in an increasingly competitive environment.
